<h2>Supplemental Information: Episode 2</h2>

  <div class="supplement" id="e5.2">
    <h3 class="supplement-title">Trauma and Vocal Utterances in Ellen Reid's <em>p r i s m</em> (2018)</h3>
    <h4>Aaron D'Zurilla (University of Cincinnati, College-Conservatory of Music)</h4>
    <h5>Release Date: Thursday, April 30, 2026</h5>
    <div class="floatsupps">
      <div style="float:left; width: 40%;">
        <img class="biopic" alt="D'Zurilla Headshot" src="../supplements/S05Ep02dzurilla.jpg">

        <p style="font-size: 12px;">

      </div>
      <div style="float:right; width: 60%;">
        <p><strong>Bio:</strong> Aaron D’Zurilla is a PhD student in Music Theory at the Cincinnati College-Conservatory of Music, and holds a Masters in Music Theory from Florida State University. As a music theorist, Aaron researches topics relating to rap, hip-hop culture, and 21st century music. With presentations at multiple regional and national conferences, previous work includes transformational and formal analyses in modern rap, a musicological study on the history of conscious rap, discussions of intersectional genres in hip-hop, and the analysis of aggregate set class networks in modern compositions.</p>

        <p><strong>Keywords:</strong> <em>opera, trauma, vocalisms, utterances, survivors</em></p>
      </div>
